contacts.responsibilities: Reviews Yellow builds. Checks that the builder followed the checklist and the tool works as described.
Reviews Orange builds. Checks security, data handling, access controls, and technical architecture.
Signs off Red builds. Verifies compliance, privacy impact, and age-appropriate design. Has authority to approve or block launch.
Answers questions about SpringBoard itself. Updates framework documents. Runs training sessions.
First call for critical incidents (data exposure, safety risk, compliance breach). Available outside business hours.

review_requirements.checks: Checklist completion, basic functionality, realistic data testing
Security, data handling, branding, access controls, third-party services
Everything above, plus compliance, privacy impact, age-appropriate design, incident response plan

response_times.time: Within 1 hour for Critical severity, 4 hours for High

role_guidance.title: Start Here: Curriculum Designers
role_guidance.content:
# Start Here: Curriculum Designers

Welcome! If you design courses, quizzes, learning pathways, or educational content at Springpod, this page is your starting point. You're probably wondering things like "Can I use AI to generate quiz questions?" or "What if it hallucinates wrong answers?" — SpringBoard has answers for all of that.

This page won't teach you everything. It points you to the right pages in the right order.

---

## Your Build Path

Most curriculum tools (quiz generators, content formatters, resource organisers) fall into the Green path. Start here to confirm yours.

  **Take the Intake Quiz**
Answer 5 questions to find your risk level and get your build path.
  **Green Build Guide**
Most curriculum tools are Green path — follow this step-by-step guide.
  **Your First Build**
Never used SpringBoard before? This walkthrough takes you through the whole process.

---

## Standards That Apply to You

Curriculum content has specific risks — hallucinated facts in educational material, safeguarding requirements for under-18s, and prompt reliability.

  **Safeguarding & Under-18s**
Critical reading if your content reaches students. Covers age-appropriate AI use and data handling.
  **Quality Standards**
Accuracy requirements for AI-generated content — especially important for educational material.
  **Prompt Management**
How to test AI outputs for hallucinations and manage prompts that generate student-facing content.

---

## Tools & Templates

Pick the right tool, check what's already approved, and start your project with a proper brief.

  **Tooling Strategy**
How to evaluate and pick the right AI tool for your use case.
  **Approved Tools**
Tools that are already pre-approved for use at Springpod.
  **Project Brief Template**
Fill this in before you start building — it takes 5 minutes and keeps you on track.

---

## Common Scenarios

**I want to generate quiz questions from course content**
This is typically a **Green path** project — internal tooling that helps you draft questions faster. However, you must test for hallucinated facts before using any AI-generated questions with students. Check the [Prompt Management](/standards/prompts) page for testing requirements, and always have a subject-matter expert review the output before it goes live.

**I'm building a student-facing content tool**
If students interact with your tool directly, this is likely a **Yellow path** (or higher) due to the under-18 audience. Take the [Intake Quiz](/intake/project-intake) to confirm, and read the [Safeguarding & Under-18s](/standards/safeguarding) standards before you start. Student-facing tools need additional review and safeguards.

**I need to review AI-generated educational content**
See the [Quality Standards](/standards/quality) page for accuracy checks and review workflows. The [Prompt Management](/standards/prompts) page covers AI testing techniques — including how to catch hallucinations, check for bias, and validate factual claims before content reaches students.

role_guidance.title: Start Here: Leadership
role_guidance.content:
# Start Here: Leadership

Welcome. If you are a director, head of department, or senior leader at Springpod, this page is your starting point. You are probably asking things like "What's our AI policy?", "What risk are we carrying?", and "How do I make budget decisions about AI tools?" — SpringBoard has answers for all of that.

This page won't teach you everything. It points you to the right pages in the right order.

---

## Policy & Risk Overview

These are the standards that govern how AI tools are built and used across Springpod.

  **Quality Standards**
What we require for AI outputs — accuracy, reliability, and review processes.
  **Security Standards**
Risk controls, compliance requirements, and data protection obligations.
  **Safeguarding & Under-18s**
Our obligations for student-facing AI under UK GDPR, DPA 2018, and ICO guidance.
  **Data Workflows**
How data is handled, classified, and protected across AI tools.

---

## Understanding the Framework

How SpringBoard classifies and manages AI projects by risk level.

  **How the Intake Works**
The 5-question classifier that assigns every project to a risk-based build path.
  **Build Paths Overview**
The risk-based tier system — Green through Red — and what each level requires.
  **When to Escalate**
Escalation paths, decision authority, and when to involve senior reviewers.

---

## Oversight & Reporting

Who is responsible for what, what tools are in use, and how to stay informed.

  **Roles & Contacts**
Who reviews what — framework contacts, technical reviewers, and approval chains.
  **Approved Tools**
The current tool landscape, what is pre-approved, and associated costs.
  **Tooling Strategy**
Cost planning, vendor evaluation criteria, and procurement guidance.
  **Updates Feed**
Latest AI developments, policy changes, and announcements relevant to Springpod.

---

## Key Questions Answered

**What's our AI risk exposure?**
The [Intake Quiz](/intake/project-intake) classifies every project by risk level. Green and Yellow projects are low-risk internal tools that need minimal oversight. Orange and Red projects require formal review before launch. See [Security Standards](/standards/security) for the specific controls applied at each level.

**How much should we budget for AI tools?**
See [Tooling Strategy](/guides/tool-selection) for cost planning frameworks and the [Approved Tools](/client-config/approved-tools) list for current spend. Most Green-path tools cost under £50/month. Yellow and above may involve platform subscriptions or API costs that need line-item budgeting.

**Are we compliant for student-facing AI?**
See [Safeguarding & Under-18s](/standards/safeguarding) for our obligations under UK GDPR, DPA 2018, and the ICO Age Appropriate Design Code. All student-facing AI requires Yellow path minimum, which includes additional review, data handling controls, and safeguarding checks before launch.

role_guidance.title: Start Here: Marketing
role_guidance.content:
# Start Here: Marketing

Welcome to SpringBoard, your guide to building AI-powered tools at Springpod. Whether you're wondering "Can I use AI for student-facing copy?" or "How do I maintain brand voice with AI?" — this page points you to the right answers. Follow the links below to navigate the parts of SpringBoard most relevant to your role.

---

## Your Build Path

  **Take the Intake Quiz**
Answer 5 questions to find your project's risk level and get matched to a build path.
  **Green Build Guide**
Most marketing tools — internal dashboards, content drafters, reporting — land here.
  **Yellow Build Guide**
If your tool produces student-facing content, you'll follow the Standard Build path.

---

## Standards That Apply to You

  **Branding Standards**
Brand voice, visual identity, and tone guidelines for AI-generated content.
  **Safeguarding & Under-18s**
Rules for any content that reaches students — age-appropriate language and safety requirements.
  **Prompt Management**
How to encode brand voice into prompts, plus AI testing and quality checks.

---

## Tools & Templates

  **Tooling Strategy**
How to pick the right content generation tool for your project.
  **Prompt Library**
Ready-to-use prompts already tuned for Springpod's brand voice and audience.
  **Approved Tools**
Pre-approved AI tools you can start using without additional sign-off.

---

## Common Scenarios

**I want to use AI to write student-facing marketing copy**
This is a **Yellow path** because the output reaches students directly. Before you start, review the [Branding Standards](/standards/branding) for voice and tone, and the [Safeguarding & Under-18s](/standards/safeguarding) standard for age-appropriate language requirements. Then follow the [Yellow Build Guide](/build-guides/yellow-standard-build).

**I'm building a social media content tool**
Internal drafting tools are typically **Green path**. Check the [Branding Standards](/standards/branding) to make sure generated content stays on-brand, and get the usual sign-off before publishing. Follow the [Green Build Guide](/build-guides/green-quick-build).

**I need AI to generate landing page copy**
If the landing page is internal-facing, this is **Green path**. If it targets under-18s, it's **Yellow path**. Either way, use prompts from the [Prompt Library](/client-config/prompt-library) for consistent brand voice, and review the [Branding Standards](/standards/branding) before going live.

role_guidance.title: Start Here: Ops & Partnerships
role_guidance.content:
# Start Here: Ops & Partnerships

You keep Springpod's employer partnerships running — onboarding, reporting, engagement tracking, and everything in between. If you've ever wondered "Can I build a dashboard to track employer engagement?" or "How do I automate reporting?" — you're in the right place. SpringBoard will get you building safely in minutes.

---

## Your Build Path

Start here to find out which process fits your project.

  **Take the Intake Quiz**
Answer 5 quick questions to find your risk level and get the right build path.
  **Green Build Guide**
Internal dashboards and reporting tools are usually Green — the fastest path.
  **Yellow Build Guide**
If your tool touches partner data or is visible to employers, start here.

---

## Standards That Apply to You

These are the standards most relevant to ops and partnerships work.

  **Data Workflows**
PII handling, data classification, and how to connect to databases safely.
  **Security Standards**
Access controls, authentication, and protecting partner data.
  **Quality Standards**
Accuracy in reporting, testing, and what "good enough" looks like.

---

## Tools & Templates

Everything you need to get started.

  **Tooling Strategy**
Pick the right platform for your project — no-code, low-code, or pro-code.
  **Approved Tools**
Pre-approved tools you can use without additional sign-off.
  **Project Brief Template**
Fill this in before you start building. It takes 10 minutes and saves hours.

---

## Common Scenarios

**I want to build an employer engagement dashboard**
Most internal dashboards are **Green path** — the simplest process. If employers or partners will see or interact with the dashboard, it moves to **Yellow path**.

Either way, check [Data Workflows](/standards/data-workflows) before connecting to any database or pulling partner data into your tool.

**I need to automate a reporting workflow**
Start by checking what data the workflow touches. If it includes employer PII (contact names, emails, contract details), review [Data Workflows](/standards/data-workflows) for classification rules.

Most internal reporting automation is **Green path**. If the output goes to partners, it's likely **Yellow**.

**I'm integrating with an external partner system**
External integrations are typically **Orange path** because they involve data flowing between systems. Review [Security Standards](/standards/security) for API and access control requirements, and file a [Review Request](/forms/review-request) before starting the build.

role_guidance.title: Start Here: Product & Engineering
role_guidance.content:
# Start Here: Product & Engineering

Welcome. You build and ship the platform — student-facing features, internal tools, integrations, and everything in between. This page maps you to the SpringBoard pages that matter most for your work, so you can answer questions like *"What standards apply to student-facing features?"* and *"When does this need pen testing?"* without searching.

---

## Your Build Path

  **Take the Intake Quiz**
Answer 5 questions to classify your project and get the right build path.
  **Orange Build Guide**
Most platform features land here — partner integrations, new user-facing screens, API work.
  **Red Build Guide**
Student-facing AI features, anything touching under-18 data at scale.

---

## Standards You Must Know

  **🛡️ Security Standards**
Pen test triggers, access controls, secret management, and API security requirements.
  **🧒 Safeguarding & Under-18s**
Mandatory for any feature students interact with — age-gating, content moderation, parental consent.
  **📊 Data Workflows**
PII classification, data warehouse requirements, retention rules, and GDPR obligations.
  **🤖 Prompt Management**
AI testing standards, prompt injection prevention, and output guardrails.

---

## Process & Templates

  **📋 Review Request**
Submit your project for architecture or security review before launch.
  **🚨 Incident Report**
If something goes wrong in production — log it here immediately.
  **📞 When to Escalate**
Not sure who to ask? This page maps common situations to escalation paths.

---

## Common Scenarios

**I'm adding AI to a student-facing feature**
Red path. This requires a safeguarding review, AI testing (prompt injection, output safety), and a pen test. Start with the [Intake Quiz](/intake/project-intake) and expect a full review cycle. Budget extra time — this is the highest-scrutiny path for good reason.

**I need to integrate a new third-party API**
Orange path minimum. Check [Security Standards](/standards/security) for API requirements (auth, rate limiting, data in transit) and file a [Review Request](/forms/review-request) before going live. If the API handles student PII, this may escalate to Red.

**I'm building an internal tool that queries the database**
Green or Yellow depending on data sensitivity. If you are querying anonymised or aggregate data, Green is fine. If the tool accesses student PII or partner data, check [Data Workflows](/standards/data-workflows) for classification — you may need Yellow or Orange controls.

escalation_path: 3. Security or data concern → Technical reviewer
4. Compliance, legal, or safety → Designated reviewer
5. Something broken in live tool → Emergency contact + Incident Report
